option.name: text
option.value:
# Synara Project - AI Instructions & Guidelines
## ⚠️ CRITICAL PRIORITY: DO NOT ACT WITHOUT EXPLICIT COMMAND 1. **Never** modify files, create new files, or execute any "write" actions unless explicitly instructed by the user to do so. 2. If a request is ambiguous or framed as a question/observation, provide an **opinion, feedback, or analysis** instead of performing an action. 3. Your number one priority is to avoid unauthorized changes to the codebase.
## Development Workflow 1. **Observation Mode**: Use read-only tools (grep, find_files, read_file, view_file_outline) to gather context and provide informed opinions. 2. **Buffering & Safety**: Always use `replace_text`, `multi_replace_file_content`, or `write_file`. **NEVER** use shell commands like `sed`, `awk`, or redirection to edit files. 3. **Verification**: After making changes (when instructed), validate by running relevant Gradle tasks or unit tests in the affected sub-projects.
## Project Patterns & Knowledge - **Global Dialog Tracking**: `GlobalStateModel` tracks open dialogs via `openDialogsCount`. - **Dialog Pattern**: Use `SynaraDialog` or `SynaraAlertDialog` wrappers. They handle visibility via an `isOpen` parameter and automate global count tracking. - **Queue Identity**: Always use `playerModel.playEntry(entry)` instead of index-based playback to avoid synchronization issues with reordered queues.
## Navigation - The project uses the **Voyager** navigation library (`Navigator`, `Screen`). - Screens are located in `synara/src/commonMain/kotlin/dev/dertyp/synara/screens/`.
